title: allium pallens subsp. coppoleri
intro: Allium pallens subsp. coppoleri, a lesser-known subspecies of the Allium genus, is a type of wild onion or garlic plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, while its herbal preparations often involve infusions, decoctions, or topical applications. In traditional medicine, Allium pallens subsp. coppoleri is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats.
